2603.21276.title: Aggregation Alignment for Federated Learning with Mixture-of-Experts under Data Heterogeneity
2603.21276.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) increasingly adopt Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architectures to scale model capacity while reducing computation. Fine-tuning these MoE-based LLMs often requires access to distributed and privacy-sensitive data, making centralized fine-tuning impractical. Federated learning (FL) therefore provides a paradigm to collaboratively fine-tune MoE-based LLMs, enabling each client to integrate diverse knowledge without compromising data privacy. However, the integration of MoE-based LLM fine-tuning into FL encounters two critical aggregation challenges due to inherent data heterogeneity across clients: (i) divergent local data distributions drive clients to develop distinct gating preference for localized expert selection, causing direct parameter aggregation to produce a ``one-size-fits-none'' global gating network, and (ii) same-indexed experts develop disparate semantic roles across clients, leading to expert semantic blurring and the degradation of expert specialization. To address these challenges, we propose FedAlign-MoE, a federated aggregation alignment framework that jointly enforces routing consistency and expert semantic alignment. Specifically, FedAlign-MoE aggregates gating behaviors by aligning routing distributions through consistency weighting and optimizes local gating networks through distribution regularization, maintaining cross-client stability without overriding discriminative local preferences. Meanwhile, FedAlign-MoE explicitly quantifies semantic consistency among same-indexed experts across clients and selectively aggregates updates from semantically aligned clients, ensuring stable and specialized functional roles for global experts. Extensive experiments demonstrate that FedAlign-MoE outperforms state-of-the-art benchmarks, achieving faster convergence and superior accuracy in non-IID federated environments.
2603.21276.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）越來越多地採用專家混合（MoE）架構，以擴展模型容量同時降低計算成本。對這些基於MoE的LLMs進行微調通常需要訪問分散且涉及隱私的數據，這使得集中式微調變得不切實際。因此，聯邦學習（FL）提供了一種範式，可以協作微調基於MoE的LLMs，使每個客戶端能夠整合多樣的知識而不妨礙數據隱私。然而，基於MoE的LLM微調整合到FL中面臨兩個關鍵的聚合挑戰，這是由於客戶端之間固有的數據異質性：（i）不同的本地數據分佈驅使客戶端發展出不同的閘控偏好以進行本地化專家選擇，導致直接的參數聚合產生一個“適合所有人”的全局閘控網絡，以及（ii）同索引的專家在客戶端之間發展出不同的語義角色，導致專家語義模糊和專家專業化的退化。為了解決這些挑戰，我們提出了FedAlign-MoE，一種聯邦聚合對齊框架，該框架共同強制執行路由一致性和專家語義對齊。具體而言，FedAlign-MoE通過一致性加權對齊路由分佈來聚合閘控行為，並通過分佈正則化來優化本地閘控網絡，保持跨客戶端的穩定性而不覆蓋區分性的本地偏好。同時，FedAlign-MoE明確量化了客戶端之間同索引專家的語義一致性，並有選擇性地聚合來自語義對齊客戶端的更新，確保全局專家的功能角色穩定且專業。大量實驗表明，FedAlign-MoE在非IID聯邦環境中超越了最先進的基準，實現了更快的收斂和更高的準確性。
2603.21276.author: Zihan Fang et.al.
2603.21276.authors: Zihan Fang, Qianru Wang, Haonan An, Zheng Lin, Yiqin Deng, Xianhao Chen, Yuguang Fang
